name: Cannonball Adderley
abstract: Julian Edwin "Cannonball" Adderley (September 15, 1928 – August 8, 1975) was an American jazz alto saxophonist of the hard bop era of the 1950s and 1960s. Adderley is perhaps best remembered by the general public for the 1966 soul jazz single "Mercy, Mercy, Mercy", which was written for him by his keyboardist Joe Zawinul and became a maj…

works_created.t: Big Man -- The Legend of John Henry

about_quotes.speaker: Tyner McCoy
about_quotes.evidence: The interviewee discusses Orrin Keepnews and mentions Cannonball Adderley only as an example of artists recorded by Keepnews on Riverside Records; no personal interaction or relationship between the interviewee and Cannonball Adderley is described.
about_quotes.doc_title: Tyner McCoy — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Gary Smulyan
about_quotes.evidence: Cannonball Adderley was huge and I don’t mean in a physical sense, I mean a huge part of my development growing up. I mean when I was playing the alto saxophone he, Charlie Parker, Phil Woods and Gene Quill, they were I mean huge influences.

about_quotes.speaker: Orrin Keepnews
about_quotes.evidence: in that period we remained close personal friends. And we saw a lot of each other, we talked to each other a lot, we shared non-musical things, and that I have to say is among the handful of things that I feel best about, in my career.
about_quotes.doc_title: Orrin Keepnews interviewed by Monk Rowe, San Francisco, California, August 7, 2002

about_quotes.speaker: Mark Soskin
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] The days of, like I’m fond of the Cannonball Adderley story where he shows up in New York and sits in with Oscar Pettiford and next thing you know he’s got a record contract.
about_quotes.doc_title: Mark Soskin interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, September 17, 2021

about_quotes.speaker: Milt Hinton
about_quotes.evidence: That was on a date with Barry Galbraith on guitar, Art Farmer on trumpet, Cannonball Adderley on saxophone, and yours truly on bass.
about_quotes.doc_title: Milt Hinton interviewed by Joe Williams, Majesty of the Seas, May 31, 1995

about_quotes.speaker: Ray Ricker
about_quotes.evidence: I had one guy that was so really good, really into Cannonball and was just Cannonball, Cannonball, Cannonball.
about_quotes.doc_title: Ray Ricker interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, January 27, 2022
